nodes.title: ASO — Agentic Search Optimization | CAPXEL | CAPXEL
nodes.content: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer IntelligenceYour Customers Are Asking AI Where to Buy. Is It Recommending You?Agentic Search Optimization - the practice of making your brand discoverable to AI agents. CAPXEL authored the standard.Test Your Site FreeTalk to Our TeamThe iceberg is real: most signal is hidden beneath the surface.The ProblemAI Agents Can't Parse Visual WebsitesMost brand websites were built for humans: visual layouts, navigation menus, interactions, and design cues. AI agents extract and verify information - and they need stable, structured signals.Audit insight~73% of brand websites are effectively invisible to LLMs.CAPXEL ASO DiagnosticsTraditional SEO optimizes for humans and search engines. ASO optimizes for the AI agents that increasingly influence what humans buy - by giving agents an authoritative source of truth.What Is ASO?Agentic Search Optimization is a new layer of discoverability: the systems that recommend brands are no longer only search engines - they are AI agents. CAPXEL coined the term ASO in November 2025.Optimized for human readersTraditional SEOFocuses on SERP rankings and human click-throughContent is written and laid out for visual scanningMetadata is often inconsistent across pagesGreat for search engines - not enough for agentsOptimized for AI agentsASO (Agentic Search Optimization)Makes your business legible to LLM-driven agentsPublishes machine-readable business intelligenceCreates consistent, verifiable structured signalsDesigned for the AI layer influencing purchase decisionsThe StandardLLM-LD: How AI Discovers BusinessesCAPXEL authored the LLM-LD standard - a machine-readable specification that defines how AI agents identify, verify, and recommend businesses.Trust SignalsStructured policy, warranty, shipping, returns, and verification signals - so agents can confidently recommend you.Entity ConsistencyOne coherent business identity across pages and channels - making it easier for agents to resolve “who you are”.Agent ReadabilityPurpose-built formats and endpoints that agents can fetch, parse, and cite - independent of UI and layout.View the LLM-LD Standard How It WorksWhite-glove implementation. We don’t “do marketing” - we give your marketing team a machine-readable foundation that agents can recognize and trust.01AuditWe scan your entire web presence - site, subdomains, key pages, and entity signals - to understand what an AI agent can (and can't) extract today.02OptimizeWe build a machine-readable intelligence layer: product, policy, location, and brand signals expressed in standardized, agent-friendly formats.03DeployWe deploy via an AI endpoint (typically ai.yourdomain.com) so agents have a reliable source of truth - versioned, consistent, and always accessible.ResultsASO outputs are measurable: structured intelligence, consistent entity signals, and an agent-ready endpoint.5Industries optimized1,956Machine-readable files generatedUp to 7Structured schemas per page60sTime to run the ASO testThe outcome: agents can answer “where to buy” with confidence.When your intelligence layer is consistent, verifiable, and easy to fetch, AI systems are more likely to include you in recommendations, comparisons, and purchase guidance.Run the Free TestTalk to CAPXELProve It's WorkingASO optimizes. BEACON proves it.Every time GPTBot, ClaudeBot, or PerplexityBot crawls your AI layer, BEACON captures it. Monthly intelligence reports show exactly which AI systems are reading your brand, how often, and what they care about. Data you literally cannot get anywhere else.Learn About BEACON ASO DiagnosticsCan AI Find Your Business? Find out in 60 seconds.CAPXEL is a white-glove data consultancy. We build the structured layer so your team can own discoverability in an AI-first world.Test Your Site FreeTalk to Our TeamRelated products: BEACON · SPYBOX · INSPEX · PROSPEXFAQEverything you need to know about Agentic Search Optimization.What is Agentic Search Optimization (ASO)?Why does ASO matter now - if I already do SEO?Where does the "73% invisible to LLMs" claim come from?What is LLM-LD?What exactly do you deploy to my domain?Who is ASO best for?How do I know ASO is actually working?

nodes.content: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer IntelligenceSee Who's Looking — Before They LeaveIdentify up to 75% of anonymous web traffic. Full identity enrichment. No opt-in required.Get StartedExplore INSPEXThe iceberg is real: most signal is hidden beneath the surface.How It WorksA Tiny Script. A Massive Lift in Visibility.SPYBOX sits quietly on your site. In under a second, it resolves a visitor’s identity and enriches them with deep consumer intelligence — so your team can activate what used to disappear.01InstallAdd a lightweight script to your site — deployed in minutes.02Identify + EnrichResolve identities in < 1 second and enrich automatically with 500+ fields.03ActivateSend profiles into your approved workflows: ads, email, analytics, or downstream CAPXEL products.FeaturesBuilt for teams who want certainty — not anonymous guesswork.Identity Capture (up to 75%)Identify a majority of anonymous traffic — turning “unknown visitors” into actionable people-level profiles.< 1 Second ResolutionFast identity resolution designed for real-time experiences and immediate downstream activation.500+ Enrichment FieldsEvery match is enriched automatically with deep consumer attributes — contact, demographics, and behavioral signals.Cross-PlatformDesigned to work with INSPEX, PROSPEX, and Buyer Ambition — capture → analyze → scale → target.Use CasesSPYBOX is the front door: it captures the signal so your team can act.E-commerce RetargetingRecover abandoned sessions by building audiences from real identities — not cookies.Ad Audience BuildingPush high-quality audiences into Meta, Google, or programmatic workflows with precision attributes.Drip Campaigns & LifecycleTrigger onboarding, winback, and nurture flows using verified identity enrichment.White-Glove Identity CaptureTurn Anonymous Traffic Into Named OpportunityWe implement SPYBOX with your governance in mind — then help your team activate the data across the stack.Get StartedExplore PROSPEXRelated: INSPEX · Buyer Ambition DataFAQCommon questions about SPYBOX.What is SPYBOX?How does SPYBOX work?What data is included in enrichment?Is SPYBOX compliant?What should I pair SPYBOX with?

nodes.title: Buyer Ambition Data — Intent Targeting | CAPXEL | CAPXEL
nodes.content: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer IntelligenceKnow Who's Ready to Buy — Before They DoClient-specific intent data, refreshed weekly, enriched with full identity profiles. Stop targeting demographics. Start targeting buyers.Talk to Our TeamSee All ProductsThe iceberg is real: most signal is hidden beneath the surface.The Problem With Intent DataEveryone Else Is Selling You Recycled AudiencesMost intent data providers run shared pools. Every client gets the same stale list of "interested" buyers — your competitor included. By the time you reach them, they've already been bombarded by everyone else using the same data.CAPXEL does it differently.What Makes Our Intent Data DifferentWhite-glove intelligence, not self-serve guesswork.Client-Specific AudiencesYour data is yours. We don't recycle audience pools across clients. Every delivery is built exclusively for your brand, your market, your goals.7-Day Fresh IdentitiesEvery weekly delivery contains people who have shown purchase intent in the past seven days. No stale data. No recycled lists. Active buyers only.Full Identity EnrichmentEvery identity comes with verified emails and mobile advertising IDs — enabling cross-device targeting across Meta, Google, programmatic, email, and direct mail.Precision Geo-TargetingNationwide, by state, by county, or hyper-local. Need buyers searching for gutter services in a specific metro? We can do that. Any vertical. Any region.Continuously RefinedWe don't set it and forget it. Using INSPEX intelligence and market sentiment analysis, we constantly refine your targeting to maximize conversion impact.B2B & B2C, All VerticalsConsumer brands, SaaS, professional services, local businesses — our intent data covers every vertical with custom aggregation for qualifying clients.How It WorksIntelligence in. Buyers out. Every week.01Intelligence IngestionWe analyze your brand, your INSPEX customer data, and market sentiment to define the exact buyer ambition verticals that will drive the highest conversion impact.02Custom Audience BuildWe source fresh, high-intent identities — people actively searching for what you sell in the past 7 days — enriched with full contact data and advertising IDs.03Weekly Delivery & RefinementYour data is delivered weekly via secure CSV. Load it directly into Meta, Google, or any ad platform. We review performance and refine targeting every cycle.Precision Targeting, Any MarketFrom product-level to brand-level. Local to nationwide. We customize the data to fit.E-Commerce BrandA supplement brand receives weekly audiences of people actively researching protein powder — enriched with emails, ad IDs, income data, and purchase behavior. Loaded directly into Meta custom audiences and email sequences. Cost per acquisition drops. ROAS compounds.Local Service BusinessA home services company targets people searching for gutter repair in their metro area. Weekly fresh audiences, geo-targeted to their service radius. Every ad dollar hits someone who's already looking. The data pays for itself in the first week.White-Glove Data ConsultancyStop Guessing. Start Targeting Buyers.The more you invest in reaching the right audience, the faster the data compounds your results. Let's build your first custom audience.Talk to Our TeamExplore INSPEXFAQEverything you need to know about Buyer Ambition Data.What is Buyer Ambition Data?How is this different from other intent data providers?What verticals does this work for?How is the data delivered?Can I target specific geographic areas?Who is Buyer Ambition Data designed for?

nodes.content: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer IntelligenceSee Every AI Agent That Visits Your BrandBEACON monitors every crawler request to your AI-optimized layer and turns it into monthly intelligence reports. Know which AI systems are reading your site, how often, and what they care about.See What AI SeesExplore ASOThe iceberg is real: most signal is hidden beneath the surface.Crawler IntelligenceProof Your ASO Investment Is WorkingAI crawler traffic is the leading indicator of agentic discovery. Know exactly where to focus your next optimization.Actionable RecommendationsCoverage gap analysis with specific next steps: which pages need richer data, which products are underrepresented, where to expand.Exclusive IntelligenceData You Can't Get Anywhere ElseNo analytics tool, no SEO platform, and no third-party estimate can tell you which AI agents are reading your brand. This data exists only at the server level - and CAPXEL gives you direct visibility into it. Every visit. Every agent. Every month.Direct server-level observabilityMonth-over-month trend trackingZero impact on site performanceBEACONSee What AI SeesKnow which AI systems are reading your brand, what they are consuming, and where your coverage gaps live.See What AI SeesExplore ASORelated: INSPEX · SPYBOXFAQCommon questions about BEACON.What is BEACON?What AI crawlers does it track?How often do I get reports?What if my site has low crawler traffic?Does BEACON slow down my site?Do I need ASO first?

nodes.content: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer IntelligenceThe 90% You Can't SeeOnly 10% of an iceberg is visible. The other 90% is what gives it the power to change everything. Data works the same way.Talk to Our TeamExplore ProductsThe iceberg is real: most signal is hidden beneath the surface.Who We AreAn AI-driven data intelligence companyCAPXEL is fundamentally an AI-driven data intelligence company and white-glove data consultancy. We don't do marketing — we empower the teams who do.Founded by Nick Dunev (Founder) and Dominick Luna (Co-Founder).Our MissionTo empower businesses with the most expansive, accurate, and secure consumer intelligence available anywhere.Our EdgeCAPXEL is built to reveal what other systems can't see.Deepest Data260M+ US identities and 500+ data points per profile — built for precision marketing intelligence.AI-NativeDesigned for the agentic era: structured intelligence, automated resolution, and scalable enrichment.LLM-LD AuthorsWe wrote the standard for how AI discovers businesses — a machine-readable layer agents can trust.Security-FirstAES-256 encryption and blockchain-based transfers. Security is infrastructure, not an add-on.The IcebergMost signal is hidden beneath the surfaceThe visible layer — clicks, sessions, and conversions — is only 10% of what drives growth. The other 90% is identity, behavior, intent, and context. We built CAPXEL to reveal it.Iceberg translationCAPXEL connects the hidden layer (identity + intent + behavior) to the visible layer (your marketing and sales execution). The result is intelligence your team can act on — without guessing.Our VisionHuman outcomes — powered by AI and deep dataA future where businesses leverage AI and deep consumer data to improve customer relationships and commerce — not in a way that feels robotic, but human.CAPXELSee what we can reveal for youStart with a conversation — we'll map the hidden signal in your market to the products that drive impact.Talk to Our TeamExplore ASORelated products: SPYBOX · INSPEX · Buyer Ambition Data · LANDMARX

nodes.title: Press | Capxel | CAPXEL
nodes.content: NewsroomPress ReleasesOfficial announcements from Capxel — the intelligence layer between brands and AI.Media inquiries: curious@capxel.comFebruary 2026TAMPA BAY, FLCapxel Launches LLM-LD, the First Open Standard for Making Websites Readable by AI AgentsNew specification gives brands a structured framework to surface in AI-powered search, recommendation engines, and autonomous agentsCapxel, the AI-native data company helping enterprises expand through intelligence-driven products, today announced the general availability of LLM-LD (Large Language Model Linked Data) — the first open standard designed to make website content natively readable by AI systems, retrieval pipelines, and autonomous agents.LLM-LD defines standardized file formats, discovery mechanisms, and conformance levels that allow any AI system to understand a website's complete content from a single index file. The specification is available under a Creative Commons BY 4.0 license at llmld.org.“JSON-LD solved machine readability for search engines. LLM-LD solves it for AI. Every major AI system — ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Claude — retrieves and synthesizes web content differently than traditional search crawlers. There was no standard for how websites should present themselves to these systems. Now there is.”“We're seeing a shift in how forward-thinking brands approach their digital presence. The companies that structure their content for AI agents today will be the ones those agents recommend tomorrow. LLM-LD gives every brand — regardless of size or technical capability — a clear path to get there.”As AI agents increasingly mediate how consumers discover products, services, and information, brands face a new visibility challenge. Recent industry research found that fewer than 1.2% of brand locations receive direct recommendations from leading AI assistants — not because the businesses lack quality, but because their content isn't structured for AI consumption.LLM-LD addresses this with a standardized index file, structured entity data, knowledge graphs, product feeds, an AI Discovery Page specification, and three conformance levels from basic discoverability to full agent-readiness.Since its launch, LLM-LD has seen rapid adoption across multiple industries including healthcare, luxury retail, professional services, and e-commerce. The companion LLM Disco Network (llmdisco.com) has attracted implementation partners across the agency ecosystem.LLM-LD is free and open. Any developer, agency, or platform can implement it without licensing fees or vendor lock-in. Capxel provides enterprise-grade implementation services for brands requiring managed deployment, ongoing optimization, and performance analytics.February 2026TAMPA BAY, FLAs AI Agents Replace Search Engines, Capxel Introduces AI Search Optimization — A New Category of Brand IntelligenceCapxel's ASO framework helps enterprises become discoverable, recommended, and cited by AI systems — before competitors realize the landscape has shiftedCapxel, the AI-native data company helping enterprises expand through intelligence-driven products, today introduced AI Search Optimization (ASO) as a defined commercial discipline — a new category of brand intelligence designed to make enterprises visible to AI agents, recommendation engines, and retrieval systems that are rapidly displacing traditional search.While the digital marketing industry remains focused on optimizing for Google's 10 blue links, a fundamental shift is underway. Consumers are increasingly turning to AI assistants — ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Claude — for purchasing decisions, service recommendations, and information discovery. These systems don't crawl and rank pages. They retrieve, synthesize, and recommend. And the vast majority of brands are invisible to them.“Every company has an SEO strategy. Almost none have an ASO strategy. The brands that show up when an AI agent answers 'What's the best product for my use case?' will capture the next decade of consumer attention. The ones that don't will wonder where their traffic went.”“This isn't a future problem. It's a current blind spot. The infrastructure to make brands readable by AI systems exists today. Most companies simply don't know it's available — or that they need it.”Industry data tells a stark story. SOCi's 2026 Local Visibility Index — the most comprehensive benchmark of multi-location brand performance, covering 350,000+ locations and 2,751 brands — added AI visibility as a core metric for the first time this year. Their findings: the gap between brands optimized for AI and those that aren't is already significant and accelerating.Capxel's ASO implementation includes structured AI layers built on the open LLM-LD standard (llmld.org), machine-readable knowledge architectures, ongoing intelligence and analytics, and white-label deployment through a growing network of agency partners.Capxel positions ASO not as a feature or add-on, but as an entirely new category of digital infrastructure — one that sits alongside SEO, paid media, and CRM as a core component of enterprise marketing architecture.“We didn't build a tool. We solved a problem no one else was addressing. Three years from now, every enterprise will have an ASO strategy the same way they have an SEO strategy today. The question is who gets there first.”About CapxelCapxel is an AI-native data company helping enterprises expand through intelligence-driven products. Founded in Tampa Bay, Florida, Capxel provides enterprise data solutions including AI Search Optimization (ASO), behavioral intelligence, identity resolution, and consumer enrichment across 260M+ profiles. Learn more at capxel.com.LLM-LD and the LLM-LD specification are released under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0).

nodes.title: Data Security | CAPXEL | CAPXEL
nodes.content: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer IntelligenceYour Data. Fort Knox.We go above and beyond industry standards. Because when you handle 260M+ identities, security isn't optional — it's foundational.Talk to Our TeamExplore ProductsThe iceberg is real: most signal is hidden beneath the surface.Security PracticesBuilt like infrastructureCAPXEL is a white-glove data consultancy. We protect what we handle with the assumption that security is not a feature — it's the foundation.AES-256 EncryptionMilitary/bank-grade encryption for data at rest and in transit.Secure TransfersEnd-to-end encryption + cryptographic splitting + one-time passwords + self-destructive links.AI AnonymizationAll PII is fully anonymized before any AI processing — protecting sensitive information by design.Internal ProtocolsSecurity-trained staff, access limitations, and strict controls across the delivery lifecycle.Our CommitmentSecurity is core infrastructure — not an add-onWe designed CAPXEL to handle sensitive identity intelligence responsibly. From encryption to access control, every layer is built to minimize risk and maximize accountability.Security-first by designFrom anonymization before AI processing to secure transfer workflows, CAPXEL treats security like critical infrastructure.Ask a questionCAPXEL SecurityQuestionsQuestions about our security practices?We'll walk you through how we protect data across encryption, transfers, anonymization, and internal controls.Talk to Our TeamView Security OverviewFAQSecurity questions we answer most often.Do you encrypt data at rest and in transit?How do you deliver files securely?Does AI ever see raw PII?Who can access customer data internally?

Collection is 93% faster — without sacrificing rigor.Built on CAPXEL infrastructureCAPXEL Security is a separate division — powered by the same deep-data AI foundations that drive our consumer intelligence products.Visit capxelsecurity.com 8 Intelligence CategoriesDOSXIER compiles and synthesizes intelligence across eight core categories — delivered as a clean, decision-ready report.Identity IntelligenceCore identity resolution, verification, and key identifiers.Contact & CommunicationsPhone, email, and communications surface area for lawful outreach and verification.Associates & NetworksRelationship mapping and contextual networks tied to the subject.Occupational FootprintEmployment, professional presence, and work-linked signals.Criminal & VehicularLawful public-record and vehicular context where applicable.Digital & DarkwebDigital footprint intelligence and exposed-signal discovery.Behavioral & PsychologicalPattern and risk signal synthesis to support protective decision-making.Threat / Geo / Cognitive AnalysisThreat context, geographic risk factors, and decision-ready prioritization.Who It's ForProfessionals who protect people and organizations — and need fast, lawful, decision-ready intelligence.Executive ProtectionDossiers that accelerate planning, route decisions, and threat screening.Private InvestigationDecision-ready subject analysis delivered fast — built for field reality.Corporate SecurityFaster, cleaner intelligence for risk teams protecting people, facilities, and operations.Law EnforcementSupportive analysis from lawful sources — designed to reduce collection time and improve clarity.Speed Meets Substance93% faster collection. <24h delivery. 100% lawful sources.DOSXIER compresses collection, verification, and synthesis into a single intelligence workflow — so teams can make faster, safer decisions.CAPXEL SecurityJoin the waitlistThis is a bridge page. The full experience lives at capxelsecurity.com.Join the Waitlist Talk to CAPXELRelated: Data Security · SPYBOX · INSPEX

nodes.title: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer Intelligence
nodes.content: CAPXEL Privacy PolicyUpdated July 25, 2024This Privacy Policy explains how CAPXEL ("CAPXEL", "we", "us", or "our") collects, uses, discloses, and protects information in connection with our data marketing services, identity and enrichment products, corporate website, and related offerings (collectively, the "Services").Table of Contents1. Information That We Collect and Use2. How We Use the Information3. How We Share Information4. Your Marketing and Opt-Out Choices5. Cookies and Similar Technologies6. Data Collected Through Our Corporate Website7. Links8. Security and Data Integrity9. Children's Privacy10. Changes to this Privacy Policy11. Storage of Information in the United States12. Data Retention13. California Privacy Rights14. Nevada Privacy RightsEU Privacy Policy (GDPR)1. Information That We Collect and UseCAPXEL provides data and marketing intelligence products and services. In connection with those Services, we may collect, receive, license, or otherwise obtain information about individuals and households from a variety of sources, including public records, partners, data licensors, and information that businesses share with us.Examples of the types of information we may collect and use include:Interests and inferred preferences, such as purchase interests, lifestyle categories, or likely affinities.Organizations and business affiliations, such as professional associations or company-level relationships where available.Public information, including information available from government records, publicly available sources, or information published online.Demographic information, such as age range, general location, household attributes, or similar segmentation data.We do not intend to collect sensitive information unless permitted by applicable law and our contracts. The exact data elements we process may vary by product, customer needs, and legal requirements.2. How We Use the InformationWe use information to provide and improve the Services, and to help our customers and partners better understand audiences, measure marketing performance, and deliver relevant content.Data Marketing ServicesWe may use information to create audience segments, analytics, and insights that customers use for marketing, advertising, lead generation, measurement, and related purposes.Online TargetingWe may help customers reach audiences across websites, apps, and channels, including by enabling targeting, suppression, attribution, and campaign measurement.Identity ProductsWe may use information to resolve identities, link signals, and maintain consistent identifiers (for example, to support privacy-safe matching and analytics).Additional Marketing ServicesWe may provide enrichment, validation, data hygiene, modeling, and other related services to help customers maintain accurate and useful datasets.To Operate Our ServicesWe also use information to operate, secure, maintain, and improve the Services; troubleshoot; perform audits; prevent fraud and abuse; and comply with legal obligations.Legal Basis for Processing (where applicable)Depending on the context and jurisdiction, we may process information under the following legal bases:Contractual Necessity — to perform contracts with customers and partners.Consent — where consent is required (for example, certain cookie or marketing choices).Legitimate Interests — to provide, secure, and improve the Services, and to support privacy-safe marketing and analytics (balanced against individual rights).Compliance — to comply with applicable laws, regulations, and lawful requests.3. How We Share InformationWe may share information in the following circumstances (subject to contractual and legal restrictions):With Customers — to deliver the Services, including audience and measurement products.With Partners — such as data licensors, platforms, and distribution partners that support or enable the Services.With Service Providers — vendors who perform services for us (e.g., hosting, security, analytics), subject to confidentiality and data protection obligations.Corporate Transfers — in connection with a merger, acquisition, financing, reorganization, bankruptcy, or sale of assets.With Affiliates — where necessary to provide the Services and operate our business.For Legal Reasons — to comply with law, regulation, court orders, subpoenas, or lawful requests; to protect rights and safety; and to prevent fraud or security incidents.4. Your Marketing and Opt-Out ChoicesYou may have choices about the use of your information for interest-based advertising or marketing.Website Opt-Out — you may opt out of certain marketing uses where we provide an opt-out mechanism on our website.DMAchoice — you may also use industry opt-out tools such as DMAchoice (where available).NAI / DAA — you can opt out of interest-based advertising from participating companies via the Network Advertising Initiative (NAI) and the Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA).Email Unsubscribe — if you receive promotional emails from us, you can unsubscribe using the link in the message.Opt-out tools are typically browser- and device-specific and may be affected by clearing cookies or using a different browser or device.6. Data Collected Through Our Corporate WebsiteWhen you visit our corporate website or contact us, we may collect information you provide directly and information collected automatically.Information we may collectContact information (such as name, email address, company, and role).Inquiry details and communications you send to us.Device and usage information (such as IP address, browser type, pages viewed, and timestamps).How we use itTo respond to inquiries and provide requested information.To operate, maintain, and improve the website.To secure the website and prevent misuse.To understand engagement and measure the effectiveness of communications.Your Data RightsDepending on your location, you may have rights regarding personal data, including: Access, Rectification, Erasure, Restrict processing, Data Portability, Object to processing, and Withdraw Consent.To exercise applicable rights, contact: legal@capxel.com. We may need to verify your request.7. LinksOur website and Services may contain links to third-party websites or services. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of those third parties. We encourage you to review their privacy policies.8. Security and Data IntegrityWe use administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect information. Measures may include encryption, hashing and tokenization techniques where appropriate, access controls, monitoring, and vendor security reviews. No system is 100% secure; we cannot guarantee absolute security.9. Children's PrivacyThe Services are not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child has provided personal information to us, contact us and we will take appropriate steps.10. Changes to this Privacy PolicyWe may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will post the updated version on this page and update the "Updated" date above.11. Storage of Information in the United StatesCAPXEL is based in the United States and may process and store information in the United States and other countries where we or our service providers operate.International Data TransfersIf you are located outside the United States, your information may be transferred to and processed in the United States, which may have different data protection laws than your jurisdiction.EEA Transfers / SCCsWhere applicable for transfers from the EEA/UK/Switzerland, we may rely on approved transfer mechanisms such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and other safeguards.12. Data RetentionWe retain information for as long as necessary to provide the Services, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, enforce agreements, and for legitimate business needs. Retention periods may vary depending on the type of information and context.13. California Privacy RightsCalifornia residents may have rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), as amended, including the right to know, delete, correct, and opt out of certain disclosures of personal information. Where applicable, CAPXEL will provide notices and processes consistent with the CCPA.14. Nevada Privacy RightsNevada residents may have rights under the Nevada Privacy Act (NPA) regarding certain sales of covered information. If applicable, you may submit a request consistent with Nevada law.EU Privacy Policy (GDPR)If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), the United Kingdom, or Switzerland, CAPXEL aims to process personal data in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applicable local laws. This includes honoring applicable data subject rights, using lawful bases for processing, and applying appropriate safeguards for international transfers.ContactQuestions about this Privacy Policy can be sent to legal@capxel.com.

nodes.title: CAPXEL — AI-Native Consumer Intelligence
nodes.content: CAPXEL Terms of ServiceUpdated October 1, 2024These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern access to and use of CAPXEL's websites, products, software, analytics, AI-enabled tools, tracking tags, and related services (collectively, the "Services"). By accessing or using the Services, you agree to these Terms. If you are using the Services on behalf of an organization, you represent and warrant that you are authorized to bind that organization.Table of ContentsPart 1: General Terms of ServicePart 2: CAPXEL Terms of Service (ToS) — Effective 1 Oct 2024Part 1: General Terms of Service1. Acceptance of TermsBy using the Services, you agree to comply with these Terms and all applicable laws and regulations. If you do not agree, do not use the Services.2. Services DescriptionCAPXEL provides data intelligence and marketing enablement services, including AI-enabled tools and products, analytics, identity resolution, enrichment, and tracking tags or scripts that may be placed on client properties to support measurement and related functionality. Services may change over time.3. User AccountsSome Services require an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of account credentials and for all activity under your account. You must promptly notify CAPXEL of any unauthorized use or security incident.4. Payment TermsFees may be subscription-based or one-time, and are as set forth in a signed order form, statement of work, master services agreement, or other written agreement with CAPXEL. Access to paid Services may require execution of a signed agreement.5. Use of ServicesYou agree to use the Services only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You will not:Use the Services to violate any law, regulation, or third-party rights.Use the Services to develop, train, or improve any AI or machine learning model, except as expressly authorized in writing by CAPXEL.Introduce malicious code, malware, or attempt to disrupt the Services.Attempt to gain unauthorized access to systems, accounts, or data.Reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to extract source code except to the extent permitted by law.6. Data Handling and PrivacyCAPXEL's processing of information is described in our Privacy Policy. Where CAPXEL provides AI-enabled features, CAPXEL intends to anonymize or de-identify personal information before using it within AI workflows, where feasible and appropriate for the feature.7. Intellectual PropertyCAPXEL and its licensors own the Services, including all software, technology, documentation, and CAPXEL data and deliverables unless otherwise stated in a signed agreement. Subject to these Terms and payment of applicable fees, CAPXEL grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Services for your internal business purposes.8. User ResponsibilitiesYou are responsible for your use of the Services, for ensuring your Authorized Users comply with these Terms, and for obtaining all necessary rights, permissions, and consents for data you provide to CAPXEL.9. TerminationCAPXEL may suspend or terminate access to the Services if you breach these Terms, if required by law, or to protect the security and integrity of the Services. Termination does not relieve payment obligations incurred prior to termination.10. Dispute ResolutionAny dispute arising from or relating to these Terms or the Services will be resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) in Florida, unless otherwise required by law. You and CAPXEL waive the right to a jury trial.11. Governing LawThese Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Florida, without regard to conflict of laws principles.12. Changes to TermsWe may update these Terms from time to time. If we make material changes, we will post the updated Terms and update the effective date. Continued use of the Services after the change becomes effective constitutes acceptance.13. Contact InformationQuestions about these Terms can be sent to legal@capxel.com.Part 2: CAPXEL Terms of Service (ToS) — Effective 1 Oct 2024This Part 2 contains additional, MSA-style terms that may apply when a customer enters into a signed agreement with CAPXEL (such as an order form, statement of work, or master services agreement). In the event of a conflict, the signed agreement controls.DefinitionsThe following definitions apply for purposes of these ToS, unless otherwise defined in a signed agreement:"AI Services" means any CAPXEL-provided features that use machine learning, large language models, automated decisioning, or similar techniques to generate outputs, recommendations, classifications, or insights."Authorized User" means an individual authorized by Client to access the CAPXEL Platform under Client's account."BACKTRAX Data" means data products, datasets, signals, identifiers, segments, or other data assets branded or designated as BACKTRAX."CAPXEL Data" means data provided by CAPXEL (including BACKTRAX Data) and any derived or aggregated data generated by CAPXEL in providing the Services, excluding Client Data."CAPXEL Platform" means the CAPXEL-hosted platform(s), dashboards, APIs, connectors, scripts, and related tooling through which the Services are delivered."CAPXEL Services" means the services and products provided by CAPXEL, including Data Services, AI Services, implementation, support, and professional services."CAPXEL Technology" means CAPXEL's software, models, methodologies, trade secrets, know-how, interfaces, and other technology used to provide the CAPXEL Services."Client Data" means data submitted to CAPXEL by or on behalf of Client (including through tags, APIs, uploads, or integrations), including event data and first-party data."Client Materials" means Client-provided content, brand assets, copy, creative, specifications, and other materials provided for use with the Services."Client Properties" means websites, apps, domains, and other digital properties controlled by Client where CAPXEL tags, scripts, SDKs, or integrations may be deployed."Data Services" means identity resolution, enrichment, analytics, audience segmentation, measurement, and related data processing services provided by CAPXEL."Deliverables" means the outputs provided to Client under an order form or statement of work, such as reports, exports, models, segments, mappings, or configurations."Documentation" means CAPXEL's user guides, API docs, specifications, and other written materials describing the Services."INSPEX", "LANDMARX", and "SPYBOX"mean CAPXEL-branded products, modules, or offerings identified by those names, as described in applicable Documentation or order forms.Scope of ServicesCAPXEL will provide the CAPXEL Services as described in the applicable order form, statement of work, or Documentation. Client is responsible for configuration choices and for ensuring use is compliant with law.Client ResponsibilitiesProvide accurate information, timely access, and cooperation as reasonably needed.Deploy tags/scripts only on Client Properties where Client has authority to do so.Maintain required notices, consents, and opt-out mechanisms for end users where applicable.Ensure Client Data is collected and shared with CAPXEL lawfully.Data HandlingCAPXEL may process Client Data to provide the Services and to maintain and improve CAPXEL Technology, subject to contract restrictions. Where feasible for AI features, CAPXEL intends to anonymize or de-identify personal information before AI processing.Intellectual Property & LicenseClient retains ownership of Client Data and Client Materials. CAPXEL retains ownership of CAPXEL Technology, CAPXEL Data, and any general-purpose improvements, methods, or learnings. Subject to the agreement and payment of fees, CAPXEL grants Client a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the CAPXEL Services and Deliverables for Client's internal business purposes.ConfidentialityEach party may receive Confidential Information from the other. Each party will protect the other's Confidential Information using reasonable care and will use it only to perform under the agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that is publicly available through no fault of the receiving party.Warranties & DisclaimersExcept as expressly stated in a signed agreement, the Services are provided "as is" and "as available". CAPXEL disclaims all implied warranties to the maximum extent permitted by law, including merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. Data and AI outputs may be probabilistic and may contain errors.Limitation of LiabilityTo the maximum extent permitted by law, CAPXEL will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for loss of profits, revenue, data, or goodwill. CAPXEL's total liability will be limited as set forth in the signed agreement or, if not specified, to the amounts paid by Client for the Services giving rise to the claim during the 12 months preceding the event.TerminationEither party may terminate as set forth in the signed agreement. Upon termination, Client's right to use the Services ends, and Client must stop using the Services and delete or return CAPXEL Confidential Information as required.Order of PrecedenceIf there is a conflict between these Terms and an executed order form or MSA, the executed agreement controls for the relevant Services.ContactFor legal inquiries: legal@capxel.com.

nodes.title: CAPXEL — AI Discovery
nodes.content:
## CAPXEL — AI Discovery

 This page provides discovery links to CAPXEL’s machine-readable AI layer. It is intended for AI crawlers, agents, and developers.

 If you followed a footer link to get here — this is the index behind the AI-Optimized badge. It helps AI systems find and understand public content. No private data, credentials, or restricted endpoints are exposed.

 ## Resources

 1. Complete site-wide machine-readable index application/json
2. Plain-text summary and link manifest text/plain
3. Full URL listing for AI-layer pages application/xml
4. Structured entity data application/json
5. Entity relationships and contextual links application/json

 ## Verification

 Pending

 Onboarding to the [LLM Disco Directory](https://llmdisco.com/directory). Conformance: Agent-Ready (Level 3).

 ## About This Standard

 AI Search Optimization (ASO)

 ASO is the practice of structuring public website content so it can be reliably discovered, understood, and cited by AI systems, retrieval pipelines, and autonomous agents. This page is part of that practice.

 LLM-LD 1.0 — Open Standard

 This AI layer is built on **LLM-LD** (Large Language Model Linked Data) — an open standard for making websites machine-readable to AI. LLM-LD defines standardized file formats, discovery mechanisms, and conformance levels so that any AI system can understand a website from a single index file.

 AI Discovery Page (ADP 1.0)

 This page itself follows the **ADP specification** — a companion standard to LLM-LD that defines how websites publish a discovery hub linking humans and machines to their AI layer.

 ## Primary Site

 [www.capxel.com](https://www.capxel.com/)

 Last updated: 2026-02-14
